reasoning: Trevor Lawrence is officially a finalist for the 2025-26 NFL MVP award. However, his odds to win upon the finalization of the candidates were listed at +15000, presenting a roughly 0.6% implied probability. He ranked lower in key statistical categories compared to the clear odds-on favorite Matthew Stafford (-180 odds, 4,707 pass yards, 46 TDs) and Drake Maye. The MVP is determined by regular-season votes cast before the playoffs begin, meaning postseason storylines have no impact. The Polymarket price of 0.00 on Jan 22 accurately reflects that Lawrence has virtually no chance to win against Stafford and Maye. Therefore, the probability is set to the absolute minimum allowed.
